summary: R-5’s ignored-file bypass and R-6’s nullish cases are fixed, and CI run 29197329127 is green at 6a801be. One medium R-6 failure path remains; no new critical issue was found.

findings.title: R-6 sanitizer can still throw while inspecting an error
findings.body: The sanitizer directly reads `code`, `stack`, and `message`, which can invoke arbitrary getters or Proxy traps. Because this occurs inside the catch block, a throwing accessor escapes before backoff and terminates the worker. Fresh Bun evaluation of the exact logic with a throwing `code` getter reproduced the failure. The committed evidence covers plain Error, primitive, and nullish values but not accessor-backed error objects.
findings.recommendation: Put all property access and coercion inside a non-throwing formatter with its own try/catch and a constant fallback. Add a red-green case using a throwing getter or revoked Proxy and assert the worker remains alive and backs off.

next_steps: Harden the worker formatter against accessor and Proxy failures, then refresh the targeted evidence.
